concepts.title: Nonsingular cubic models
concepts.blurb: A Weierstrass equation $y^2=x^3+ax+b$ defines an elliptic curve when $\Delta=-16(4a^3+27b^2)\neq0$. Nonsingularity is exactly what lets geometry and arithmetic interact cleanly.

concepts.title: Rational points and the group law
concepts.blurb: Secant–tangent addition makes $E(\mathbb{Q})$ an abelian group with identity at infinity. Arithmetic questions become structural questions about this group of rational points.

concepts.title: Mordell–Weil finiteness
concepts.blurb: Mordell–Weil says $E(\mathbb{Q})\cong E(\mathbb{Q})_{\mathrm{tors}}\oplus\mathbb{Z}^r$. The finite rank $r$ is the key invariant linked conjecturally to $L(E,s)$ at $s=1$.
